summary_of_statement: Reported seeing a man lying near the steps on the evening of 30 November and later communicating with Constable Moss after seeing the body next morning.
reliability_issues: OCR does not clearly preserve witness name on page 15; identity requires visual/manual check.
contradictions: Cannot identify man by face; says definitely same person but source also records no facial identification.
follow_up_questions: Confirm full name from page image and official transcript; compare with existing person-register lead only after visual confirmation.

role: Police constable / first responding police witness
summary_of_statement: Described attending Somerton Beach after a telephone message, body position, cigarette near collar/cheek, lack of visible violence, transport to Royal Adelaide Hospital and City Morgue, and initial property search.
reliability_issues: OCR readable but should be checked against image for exact wording.
contradictions: Did not find Tamam Shud slip in initial search.
follow_up_questions: Clarify exact time of clothing search and transport sequence.

role: Municipal Tramways Trust claims officer assistant
summary_of_statement: Identified bus ticket as MTT ticket issued by conductor Holdernesse on 30 November 1948 on route departing Railway Station at 11.15 a.m.
reliability_issues: Ticket evidence identifies issue context, not passenger identity.
follow_up_questions: Confirm serial number and route from page image.

summary_of_statement: Stated he had no independent recollection of the November trip apart from records/log and Hall statement.
contradictions: No recollection of passenger load or individual holder.
follow_up_questions: Confirm exact name and statement date from page image.

summary_of_statement: Described making a mould and cast of the deceased features at the City Mortuary on 7 June 1949 and observations about body/feet.
reliability_issues: Some observations are assumption-based and should be separated from physical description.
contradictions: Assumptions about footwear/body habitus are not direct identification evidence.

summary_of_statement: Reported arriving at Somerton about 7.20 or 7.30 p.m. on 30 November with Miss Neill, seeing a man lying on the beach, and observing no movement before leaving around 8 p.m.
reliability_issues: Witness saw limited body area and evening light conditions.

summary_of_statement: Described postmortem examination at City Mortuary at 7.30 a.m. on 2 December 1948, external observations, organ congestion, stomach contents, and medical opinion.
reliability_issues: OCR has medical terminology errors; page-image verification critical.
contradictions: Medical opinion limited by negative toxicology and uncertainty over poison/natural cause.

summary_of_statement: Received stomach contents, liver/muscle, urine and blood on 2 December 1948 and tested for common poisons without detecting any.
reliability_issues: Strong OCR on key page but chemical terms need manual check.
contradictions: Evidence limits claim to common poisons and submitted specimens.

summary_of_statement: Was with Strapps on evening 30 November, saw a person on beach, thought something might be wrong, did not see legs move, and remarked the man might be dead.
reliability_issues: Single-page OCR readable; name OCR has minor distortion.

summary_of_statement: Produced luggage ticket associated with suitcase; stated ticket was issued 30 November 1948 at 11 a.m. stamp window and suitcase remained unclaimed.
contradictions: Did not issue ticket; actual issuer absent on holidays.
follow_up_questions: Identify issuer Craig and exact luggage ticket number.

summary_of_statement: Described identification inquiries, clothing and body property, suitcase recovery on 14/19 January, contents, removed tags, weather, circulation of description/fingerprints/photos, and recalled exhibit custody.
reliability_issues: Long OCR section with many distorted words; manual transcription critical.
contradictions: Contains many custody and inventory claims needing item-level confirmation.

summary_of_statement: Photographed body full face and side face, fingerprinted deceased, circulated photographs/fingerprints, and photographed paper found on deceased.
reliability_issues: OCR readable with name distortion; exact exhibit numbers require visual check.
contradictions: No identification resulted according to OCR passage.
follow_up_questions: Confirm exhibit numbers C.5-C.8 and photo of paper.

summary_of_statement: Gave train arrival/departure times for 30 November 1948 including Melbourne Express and Henley Beach departures.
reliability_issues: OCR readable but train timetable values should be checked.
contradictions: Timetable does not prove deceased travelled on any train.
follow_up_questions: Check all train times against page image.

summary_of_statement: Compared clothing in suitcase/body, reported sand in trouser cuffs, received Tamam Shud slip from Leane, made bookshop/library inquiries about Rubaiyat text and meaning.
reliability_issues: OCR generally readable but proper nouns/technical book details need checking.
contradictions: Could not find copy with words torn from it in searched sources per OCR.

summary_of_statement: Stated he issued the Henley Beach ticket on 30 November during his 6.15 a.m. to about 2 p.m. shift and could not remember purchaser.
reliability_issues: Good OCR but exact railway initials need check.
contradictions: Purchaser not identified; exact issue time impossible from testimony.

summary_of_statement: Compared clothing and suitcase contents, thread, tags, cleanliness, body condition, and gave medical opinion regarding unnatural death/poison possibilities.
reliability_issues: OCR contains medical and technical distortion; requires manual transcription.
contradictions: Some statements are opinion/assessment, not direct observation.

summary_of_statement: Reviewed medical facts and Dwyer/Bennett evidence; discussed possible poison groups and timing difficulties.
reliability_issues: OCR has heavy chemical/medical distortion; manual transcription critical.
contradictions: Speculative poison discussion must not be converted into identified poison.
follow_up_questions: Transcribe full testimony and preserve uncertainty.

summary_of_statement: Examined body at 9.40 a.m. on 1 December 1948 in police ambulance outside Adelaide Hospital and estimated death could have occurred up to eight hours earlier.
reliability_issues: OCR readable but should be checked against image.
contradictions: Estimate based on cursory examination/rigor mortis per OCR.
